title: Rederive log entry
description: One JSON object per line in context/rederive_log.jsonl, appended once per session after re-deriving the standing questions against the current repo. This is the recording layer that makes the staleness and acknowledge-without-rederive metrics computable. The log is append-only; entries are never edited or deleted.

kind.description: Discriminator. Tools that share this file with other entry kinds must skip lines whose kind is not 'rederive'.

sid.description: Session identifier. One rederive entry per session is the protocol; mid-session re-grounding passes reuse the same sid.

repo_head_sha.description: git rev-parse HEAD at rederivation time. This is what makes the acknowledge-without-rederive detector computable: all-delta:false is only suspicious when HEAD moved.

results.description: One result per question actually re-derived this session. Questions not re-derived are simply absent — their absence is what the staleness metric counts.

q_id.description: id of the question in the standing questions store.

last_rederived_ts.description: When this question was last actually re-derived — normally equal to the entry's ts.

delta.description: true if re-derivation produced a materially different answer than the previously recorded one; false if the answer is unchanged. First-ever derivation of a question records delta:false with a note.

note.description: What changed (for delta:true) or anything worth recording. The reference protocol (skill) treats a missing note on a delta:true result as a protocol violation; this schema cannot express that constraint, so it remains optional here. Never quote secrets in a note — the log is typically committed and never pruned.
